title: DB migration: remove request_feature_fix, start_expert_session, create_project_rule from all roles
spec:
Create supabase/migrations/216_remove_mcp_tools_batch3.sql.

This migration removes three MCP tools from the mcp_tools array of every role in the roles table:
- request_feature_fix
- start_expert_session
- create_project_rule

Follow the same pattern as the most recent migration that removes MCP tools (e.g. 211_remove_batch_create_jobs_from_roles.sql or 212_remove_write_mcp_tools_from_roles.sql). Use array removal syntax on roles.mcp_tools to remove all three tool names.

Do NOT touch supabase/functions/request-feature-fix/index.ts — the edge function stays as-is.

Commit the migration file to the current branch.
acceptance_tests:
Given any agent prompt is generated
When the prompt is inspected
Then it does NOT contain request_feature_fix MCP tool
And it does NOT contain start_expert_session MCP tool
And it does NOT contain create_project_rule MCP tool

Given the migration is applied
When roles are queried
Then no role has request_feature_fix, start_expert_session, or create_project_rule in its mcp_tools array

title: Remove request_feature_fix, start_expert_session, create_project_rule handler blocks from agent-mcp-server.ts
spec:
Remove three tool handler blocks from packages/local-agent/src/agent-mcp-server.ts:

1. request_feature_fix — handler block starting around line 1327 (the guardedHandler block for 'request_feature_fix' including its tool name registration)
2. start_expert_session — handler block starting around line 1282 (the guardedHandler block for 'start_expert_session' including its tool name registration)
3. create_project_rule — handler block starting around line 500 (the guardedHandler block for 'create_project_rule' including its tool name registration)

For each, remove:
- The string literal tool name (e.g. "request_feature_fix") in the tools array
- The entire guardedHandler(...) call block including schema and handler function

Do NOT touch the edge function or any other files. Ensure the file remains syntactically valid TypeScript after the removals.

Commit the change to the current branch.
acceptance_tests:
Given the MCP server is running
When an agent calls request_feature_fix
Then the call is rejected (tool not registered)

Given the MCP server is running
When an agent calls start_expert_session
Then the call is rejected (tool not registered)

Given the MCP server is running
When an agent calls create_project_rule
Then the call is rejected (tool not registered)

Given packages/local-agent/src/agent-mcp-server.ts is compiled
When TypeScript compilation runs
Then no compile errors are produced

title: CLI: add start-expert-session command
spec:
Create packages/cli/src/commands/start-expert-session.ts and register it in packages/cli/src/index.ts.

## New file: packages/cli/src/commands/start-expert-session.ts

Follow the same structure and patterns as packages/cli/src/commands/create-feature.ts (HTTP helper, credential loading, flag parsing, --help output, JSON stdout, process.exit).

Flags:
- --company <uuid>        required — company ID
- --role-name <string>    required — role to start (e.g. test-deployment-expert)
- --brief <string>        required — session brief
- --machine-name <string> required — defaults to "auto" if not provided
- --project-id <uuid>     required — project to scope the session
- --headless              optional boolean flag (no value, presence = true)
- --batch-id <string>     optional — batch ID to associate session with

--help must print usage with all flags documented and exit 0.

Call POST {supabaseUrl}/functions/v1/start-expert-session with the body:
{ company_id, role_name, brief, machine_name, project_id, headless, batch_id }

Include Authorization and apikey headers (same as create-feature.ts pattern). Print JSON response to stdout. On HTTP error, write error JSON to stderr and exit 1.

## Register in packages/cli/src/index.ts

Import the new command and add a case 'start-expert-session': in the switch router, same pattern as existing commands. Also add it to the help output list.

Commit to the current branch.
acceptance_tests:
Given the CLI is available
When running zazig start-expert-session --help
Then usage info is printed with all flags documented (--company, --role-name, --brief, --machine-name, --project-id, --headless, --batch-id)
And exit code is 0

Given the CLI is available
When running zazig start-expert-session --company {uuid} --role-name test-deployment-expert --brief "test" --machine-name auto --project-id {uuid}
Then the edge function POST /functions/v1/start-expert-session is called
And an expert session is started

Given packages/cli/src/commands/start-expert-session.ts exists
When TypeScript compilation runs
Then no compile errors are produced

title: CLI: add create-project-rule command
spec:
Create packages/cli/src/commands/create-project-rule.ts and register it in packages/cli/src/index.ts.

Note: packages/cli/src/commands/create-rule.ts already exists and calls the same edge function but uses --rule instead of --rule-text. The new command is a separate entry point with the correct flag name per spec.

## New file: packages/cli/src/commands/create-project-rule.ts

Follow the same structure and patterns as packages/cli/src/commands/create-rule.ts (HTTP helper, credential loading, flag parsing, --help output, JSON stdout, process.exit).

Flags:
- --company <uuid>             required — company ID
- --project-id <uuid>          required — project to add the rule to
- --rule-text <string>         required — the rule text (e.g. "Always use TypeScript")
- --applies-to <string>        required — comma-separated list (e.g. "code,combine")

--help must print usage with all flags documented and exit 0.

Call POST {supabaseUrl}/functions/v1/create-project-rule with body:
{ company_id, project_id, rule_text, applies_to }

where applies_to is parsed as a comma-separated array.

Include Authorization, apikey, x-company-id headers (same as create-rule.ts). Print JSON response to stdout. On HTTP error, write error JSON to stderr and exit 1.

## Register in packages/cli/src/index.ts

Import the new command and add a case 'create-project-rule': in the switch router. Also add it to the help output list.

Commit to the current branch.
acceptance_tests:
Given the CLI is available
When running zazig create-project-rule --help
Then usage info is printed with all flags documented (--company, --project-id, --rule-text, --applies-to)
And exit code is 0

Given the CLI is available
When running zazig create-project-rule --company {uuid} --project-id {uuid} --rule-text "Always use TypeScript" --applies-to "code,combine"
Then the edge function POST /functions/v1/create-project-rule is called
And a project rule is created

Given packages/cli/src/commands/create-project-rule.ts exists
When TypeScript compilation runs
Then no compile errors are produced

title: Prompt layer updates: create-project-rule to UNIVERSAL_PROMPT_LAYER, start-expert-session to CPO prompt
spec:
Update three files to document the new CLI commands for agents.

## 1. supabase/functions/_shared/prompt-layers.ts — UNIVERSAL_PROMPT_LAYER

Find the CLI Write Commands section in UNIVERSAL_PROMPT_LAYER. Add `zazig create-project-rule` to that section following the same format as the existing CLI write commands already listed. Include: command name, required flags (--company, --project-id, --rule-text, --applies-to), a brief description, and example invocation.

## 2. CPO role prompt file

Find the CPO personality/role markdown file (search for the file that defines the CPO system prompt or personality — likely in supabase/functions/_shared/ or a prompts directory). Add a new section documenting:

```
zazig start-expert-session
  --company <uuid>        Company ID
  --role-name <string>    Role to start (e.g. test-deployment-expert)
  --brief <string>        Session brief
  --machine-name <string> Machine to run on (default: auto)
  --project-id <uuid>     Project scope
  --headless              Run without TUI
  --batch-id <string>     Optional batch association
```

Include a usage example. This section goes in the CPO prompt ONLY — NOT in UNIVERSAL_PROMPT_LAYER.

## 3. CPO CLAUDE.md

Find the CPO CLAUDE.md file (search for CLAUDE.md files under agent/exec/cpo directories or similar). Update it to:
- Remove any references to the start_expert_session MCP tool
- Add the equivalent zazig start-expert-session CLI command with flags and example
- Remove any references to the create_project_rule MCP tool  
- Add a note that create-project-rule is now a CLI command (zazig create-project-rule)
- Remove any references to request_feature_fix MCP tool

Commit all changes to the current branch.
acceptance_tests:
Given a CPO agent prompt is generated
When the prompt is inspected
Then it contains zazig start-expert-session CLI docs
And it does NOT contain start_expert_session MCP tool

Given any non-CPO agent prompt is generated
When the prompt is inspected
Then it contains zazig create-project-rule CLI docs in UNIVERSAL_PROMPT_LAYER
And it does NOT contain create_project_rule MCP tool
And it does NOT contain start-expert-session CLI docs

Given any agent prompt is generated
When the prompt is inspected
Then it does NOT contain request_feature_fix MCP tool
